title: Go 1.26 Toolchain, Programs, Packages, and Evidence · Mastery Check
summary: A small operations team needs a reproducible status tool, but its source, package boundary, formatter, build, runtime, and release identity are unclear. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a reproducible first-program and toolchain evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain how source files, package declarations, imports, init order, and package main produce an executable program.
Choose and interpret go run, build, install, test, fmt, vet, doc, env, version, and help evidence for a bounded task.
Apply UTF-8 source, identifiers, keywords, tokens, semicolon insertion, comments, and formatting rules without relying on visual coincidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Program Package Main
steps.instruction:
Order the Program Package Main evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Program Package Main.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Program Package Main assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Program Package Main, keep Program Package Main connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A quiz status command is tested with Go 1.26.5 for linux/amd64 and the browser Yaegi subset. Predict package initialization, output, artifact identity, and which go fmt, test, vet, build, env, doc, or version evidence is still missing before release 3. Competency probe: Explain how source files, package declarations, imports, init order, and package main produce an executable program.
